purpose: Publish a structured capability manifest for external agents describing available tools, prompts, workflows, auth requirements, and write scopes.

endpoints.description: Return a machine-readable capability manifest for authenticated or public agents.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Machine-readable capability manifest provider for external agents.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a platform should tell external agents what tools and workflows exist before they make calls.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as the execution layer itself or as a substitute for fine-grained authorization checks on actual tool calls.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Client fetches /agent/capabilities. 2) It selects tools and prompts based on the manifest. 3) It calls the corresponding execution endpoints with the required auth.

failure_modes.condition: Manifest drifts from real runtime capabilities
failure_modes.symptom: Agents discover tools that are missing or call signatures that are wrong
failure_modes.recovery: Generate the manifest from the same source of truth used to mount tools and workflows

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Model manifest entries with stable ids, human descriptions, auth requirements, input shapes, and write scopes. Generate from a typed registry rather than handwritten string blobs.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Capability manifests become stale quickly if they are edited separately from route and tool definitions. Public and authenticated visibility can differ by tool.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep the manifest read-only and derive it from the runtime capability registry.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not hardcode capabilities in multiple places and do not expose secret configuration in manifest output.
